messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "bundle-pricing-kills-bakeoffs",
 "title": "Near-zero-margin PDU bundling inside power+cooling packages means the contestable Year-1 pool is below the model's own 0.5% floor",
 "narrative": "The obtainableFactor band (0.5–3%) assumes there exists a pool of standalone rack-PDU purchase decisions a new entrant can compete for. But the same incumbents sell the $23B power and $26B cooling packages those PDUs plug into, and the procurement record shows PDUs travelling as embedded line items inside those packages — where the incumbent can price the PDU at near-zero margin because the busway, UPS and cooling carry the deal. The only standalone PDU procurements surfaced by research were tiny ($58K–$130K), had 1–2 bidders, and one was explicitly sole-sourced. Meanwhile the 1%-of-SAM anchor itself is generic startup folklore: the sources define SOM as a 1–3 or 3–5 year cumulative window, and the closest worked hardware comparable runs at ~0.1% of SAM. Both legs of the 1% — the benchmark and the tender evidence — point below the band, not inside it.",
 "category": "execution",
 "targetNodes": [
  "obtainableFactor"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "Incumbents holding both the power ($23B) and cooling ($26B) packages bundle intelligent PDUs into integrated deals, cross-subsidising PDU price to zero incremental margin — no bake-off is ever held on the PDU line",
   "refs": [
    "digest:cust-mix#c2",
    "digest:cust-mix#c3",
    "ledger:shape.cr3"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "The residual standalone-tender pool is small and often non-contestable (sole-sourced, 1–2 bidders), while the 1% Year-1 benchmark the node anchors on is defined by its own sources as a multi-year cumulative figure — Prospeo frames SOM as 1–3 years, Wise as 3–5, and TryBuildCo's worked example lands at ~0.1% of SAM",
   "refs": [
    "digest:obtainable-benchmark#c1",
    "digest:obtainable-benchmark#c6",
    "digest:obtainable-benchmark#c7"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "True Year-1 obtainable share of SAM falls to ~0.3% — below the band's 0.5% floor — because the denominator (SAM) includes bundled demand that never becomes a contestable decision, and the Tractian cross-check that supported ≤1% is itself unverified against its source ($200K starting revenue, not $1.2M first-year)",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:obtainableFactor",
    "digest:obtainable-benchmark#c10",
    "digest:obtainable-benchmark#c0"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "The founder's team already 'corrected' this node once (3%→1%) and the band looks conservative, so it reads as the model's most stress-tested number. The hidden failure is compositional: the band debates WHICH percentage of SAM is winnable, while the bundling mechanism removes demand from the contestable denominator entirely — a smaller pie of decisions, not a smaller slice of spend.",
 "expectedObviousness": "medium",
 "falsifier": "A logged pipeline of ≥5 standalone, multi-vendor rack-PDU tenders in CE-7, each ≥€250K, posted within any 6-month window, in which a non-incumbent vendor is qualified to bid.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.4,
  "rationale": "The bundling evidence (integrated contracts, sole-sourced standalone bids) and the benchmark-window semantics both push the same direction, but retrofit and colo tenant purchases do generate some genuinely standalone PDU decisions that the tender search may have under-sampled.",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "obtainableFactor",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.003,
   "note": "Contestable-decision pool shrinks the effective Year-1 capture below the modeled 0.5% floor"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Ratio of bundled vs standalone rack-PDU procurement in published CE tenders",
   "where": "TED (tenders.europa.eu) — compare PDU-specific CPV lots vs PDU lines inside integrated electrical-fit-out lots",
   "threshold": "≥80% of PDU volume appearing only inside integrated lots over two quarters",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Incumbent commentary on solutions/portfolio attach rates for rack power",
   "where": "Vertiv and Schneider quarterly earnings calls; Schneider capital markets day materials",
   "threshold": "Explicit statements that rack PDU is sold predominantly as part of integrated rack/power/cooling solutions",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Build a bottom-up contestable-tender census for CE-7 (12 months of TED + colo tenant RFQs) to replace the generic 1% benchmark with a counted pipeline",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "obtainableFactor",
   "note": "Directly converts the node from folklore benchmark to observed decision volume"
  },
  {
   "action": "Target the purchase moments bundles don't reach: colo TENANT-side PDU purchases and brownfield retrofit/metering upgrades, where no incumbent package exists to hide the PDU inside",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Retrofit demand is decision-rich even when new-build demand is bundled"
  },
  {
   "action": "Price and package for attach to third-party rack integrators' bundles rather than head-to-head against incumbent bundles",
   "type": "operational",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": null
  }
 ],
 "lens": "competitive-foreclosure",
 "riskId": "risk.competitive-foreclosure.bundle-pricing-kills-bakeoffs"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

Meanwhile the 1%-of-SAM anchor itself is generic startup folklore: the sources define SOM as a 1–3 or 3–5 year cumulative window, and the closest worked hardware comparable runs at ~0.1% of SAM. Schneider capital markets day materials","threshold":"Explicit statements that rack PDU is sold predominantly as part of integrated rack/power/cooling solutions","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Build a bottom-up contestable-tender census for CE-7 (12 months of TED + colo tenant RFQs) to replace the generic 1% benchmark with a counted pipeline","type":"information","voiNodeId":"obtainableFactor","note":"Directly converts the node from folklore benchmark to observed decision volume"},{"action":"Target the purchase moments bundles don't reach: colo TENANT-side PDU purchases and brownfield retrofit/metering upgrades, where no incumbent package exists to hide the PDU inside","type":"strategic","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Retrofit demand is decision-rich even when new-build demand is bundled"},{"action":"Price and package for attach to third-party rack integrators' bundles rather than head-to-head against incumbent bundles","type":"operational","voiNodeId":null,"note":null}]}
